programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Charity Wakefield and Hattie Morahan lead the cast in Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel about a voyage of sexual and romantic discovery. Following the death of her husband, Mrs Dashwood and her daughters are forced to give up their home and move to a small cottage in Devonshire. Elinor is disappointed to leave her old home after falling in love with Edward Ferrars, her sister-in-law's brother, but looks forward to a promised visit. Marianne quickly finds she has an admirer in Colonel Brandon but is swept of her feet by neighbour Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Colonel Brandon is called away on urgent business, leaving Marianne free to fall blissfully in love with Willoughby - until he too suddenly leaves for London. Meanwhile, Elinor has a disappointing meeting with Edward, but the reasons for his distant behaviour become clear when Sir John's niece Lucy Steele entrusts her with a shocking secret(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Marianne's health deteriorates as Colonel Brandon reveals the full extent of Willoughby's betrayal. Meanwhile, Edward states his intentions to honour his engagement to Lucy Steele, despite being disinherited by his mother as a result. Charity Wakefield and Dominic Cooper star(n)

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Christmas special from 1995. The Meldrews' wedding anniversary celebrations turn sour as they learn cousin Ursula has died while watching Noel Edmonds - forcing Victor to defy a witch's grim warning and set out to put her affairs in order(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation when he is told during a confession that Bishop Talbot will be assassinated tomorrow. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The second of a two-part story picks up where things left off last week, with Humphrey, Florence and Dwayne having swapped the sun and sand of the Caribbean for the cold and noise of London. Their latest investigation has taken them back to the detective inspector's home town - but they've hit a brick wall with the discovery of another dead body. And now they have two murders to solve. Meanwhile, Dwayne has an emotional encounter and Humphrey receives advice from an unexpected source that helps him reach a big decision. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall, Danny John-Jules and Josephine Jobert, with guest appearances from Ardal O'Hanlon and Sally Bretton(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sasha is forced to work with her ex-husband Ned Hancock when their teams investigate the death of film critic Oliver Houghton, whose body was discovered floating in the Thames. UCOS delves into the victim's past and explores his link to the murder of a conceptual artist 20 years previously, while Hancock's knowledge of London's hidden past proves invaluable when a piece of occult footage from the 1970s plunges the investigators into the macabre world of human sacrifice. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance by Barnaby Kay(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A woman's severed leg is found in the grass at the side of a motorway and Taggart and Livingstone are brought into the investigation. As the body parts build up Taggart finds himself no closer to the solution and the investigation leads them to a butcher who becomes a prime suspect. However, when he turns up dead, killed with the same gun as the dismembered corpse, it appears to be another dead end(n)

Jack Davenport and Justine Waddell star(n)

programme.title: Wuthering Heights
programme.desc: First in a two-part adaptation of Emily Bronte's classic tale. In 1815, Mr Earnshaw returns home from Liverpool with orphan Heathcliff, who befriends his daughter Cathy but is resented by his son Hindley. Upon his father's death 10 years later, Hindley inherits Wuthering Heights and forces Heathcliff to live in the stables, while Cathy must choose between a life of comfort and her love for Heathcliff. Tom Hardy, Charlotte Riley, Andrew Lincoln and Sarah Lancashire star(n)

programme.title: Wuthering Heights
programme.desc: Conclusion. Tormented by his love for Cathy, Heathcliff arrives at Thrushcross Grange the day after her wedding to Edgar. Tension mounts as the pair acknowledge their passion for one another, but when she falls pregnant with Edgar's child, Heathcliff leaves Gimmerton to marry Isabella. Tom Hardy, Charlotte Riley, Andrew Lincoln, Rosalind Halstead and Burn Gorman star in Emily Bronte's classic tale(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Mike Shepherd is sent to the New Zealand town of Brokenwood to investigate the death of a farmer, after his body is found by two local fishermen. As he uncovers a family's tragedies and secrets, he also learns that Brokenwood is a place where shadows lurk just beneath the surface. Drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson(n)

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: What starts as a simple case of a missing horse leads the team into the gardens of Toronto's elite in pursuit of a murderous jewel thief(n)

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Kenneth Branagh returns to his Bafta-winning role of the Swedish detective in three adaptations of stories by Henning Mankell. Wallander moves into a new home with his girlfriend, only for their dreams of domestic bliss to be shattered when they discover a skeleton in the garden. He must find the killer if he has any hopes of getting his life settled again - and is surprised to discover the body was buried more recently than he first thought. Saskia Reeves and Sarah Smart co-star(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry is beside himself with worry about his nephews' safety, convinced they are being abused by his brother, and jeopardises the case to help them. Questioning his own mortality in the process, he wonders whether a quieter pace of life would suit him better, but soon realises he would miss the thrill of the job and sets off home in a more positive mood - until he gets a shooting pain down his arm(n)

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gina orders Kerry to sever her ties with the Radford family or leave the Met. programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Patricia Routledge stars as Lancashire housewife Hetty Wainthropp, a part-time Post Office worker with a sideline in sleuthing. In this first episode, the mysterious death of a pensioner leaves a few unanswered questions, but Hetty smells a rat and with her young assistant Geoffrey, sets about solving the mystery the police have little interest in. Drama, also starring Dominic Monaghan and David Benfield(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty and amiable sidekick Geoffrey become embroiled in a convoluted case involving a deaf birdwatcher who goes missing at the same time as a woman's body is found at the bottom of a cliff he is known to frequent. Light-hearted drama, with Patricia Routledge and Dominic Monaghan, featuring a guest appearance by David Bower(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The owners of a Tuscan inn are sent a severed finger and a photo of their son. Convinced he has been kidnapped by the Mafia, their daughter hires Hetty while she and Robert are on holiday(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives move into their new premises following the explosion that wrecked the old Nonnatus House, and are joined by fresh arrival Sister Winifred - a kindly-but-naive young woman. Jenny becomes concerned about new mother Merle's mental state and the welfare of her baby, who isn't feeding properly. Dr Turner believes it could be something more serious - although the mystery is eventually solved by the last person anyone would have expected. Chummy is struggling to adjust to her new role as a housewife and mother, and organises an open day at the community centre. Jessica Raine, Stephen McGann and Miranda Hart star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie's feathers are ruffled when Jenny is promoted, but the new Acting Sister has more to worry about after a mother-to-be reveals the baby she is carrying was not fathered by her husband - and to complicate matters, the real father is black. Cynthia is inspired by a lecture on natural childbirth, convinced it will help an overly anxious patient, while Alec and his friends try to persuade Jenny to join them on a CND march. Jessica Raine, Helen George and Bryony Hannah star in the 1950s-set nursing drama(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A reluctant Brian enters an addiction centre run by monks to treat his alcoholism, but soon becomes bored and looks for a distraction. As Jack, Clarissa and Thomas share concerns about Nikki's wellbeing, a body found in a reservoir is linked to a murder case from 16 years previously. programme.title: Waking the Dead

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore obtains three items from a tip in Surrey. Creative duo Josh and Oli are masters of woodwork, but will they be left scratching their heads when Sarah drops off a pile of old floorboards? programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture restorer William Kirk takes on a cherished family rocking horse that is in desperate need of restoration. The paint work and plaster is flaking away and the leather work is tattered and torn, but saddler maker Suzie Fletcher is also on hand. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay works wonders on a Royal Doulton plaque, which was broken in two during a house move 57 years ago and has remained hidden in a drawer ever since. Plus, Brenton West works on a Victorian stereoscopic viewer. These were a popular form of entertainment for Victorians and make images appear in 3D for the viewer(n)

programme.title: Pointless

John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport

She and regular team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson take a trip down memory lane as they are joined by three distinguished past guests in four-time Major-winning golfer Laura Davies, the most prolific try-scorer in English rugby league history Martin Offiah, and triple world champion gymnast Beth Tweddle, as well as former Rangers and Scotland striker Ally McCoist, who was once a team captain on the show(n)

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a Second World War drama, rarely performed because of its historical inaccuracy and poor research. Director Chris plays Rufus Heal, a dashing pilot reduced to a desk job cracking German codes in a top secret Allied facility after losing his leg. He is assisted by Sandra as uptight Englishwoman Valerie Sky and Vanessa as French codebreaker Camille, under the watchful eye of Annie as Wing Commander Wickham. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Having successfully made landfall in Whitby, Count Dracula heads for the gleaming lights of London where he finds a new environment pulsing with fresh blood. He lays his plans to spread his foul vampire contagion, focusing his sights on Lucy Westenra, a beautiful and popular girl with no shortage of young men in her thrall. However, he finds his plans unexpectedly checked by an alliance of formidable foes. Claes Bang, John Heffernan, Dolly Wells, Joanna Scanlan and Morfydd Clark star in the final instalment of Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss' adaptation of the Bram Stoker classic(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The day of Godmother and Dad's wedding arrives and while Jake wants to know where Claire is, the Priest manages to upstage the bride. As secrets are revealed and decisions are made, will Fleabag find the ending she is looking for? Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott, Sian Clifford, Bill Patterson and Olivia Colman(n)

programme.title: Miranda

A whole host of treats and surprises are lined up as they look back over a decade of fun, friendship, romance and all that made millions of fans connect with this unique and very special sitcom. With Miranda at the helm, they also share some of their fondest memories of the show, along with a series of behind-the-scenes tales(n)

In a new twist, one dance captain will be able to pick an act and take them straight through to the live shows, and Love Island star and professional dancer Curtis Pritchard joins as the new receptionist, looking after all the acts backstage(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dylan decides it is time to take a stand, both in his personal and professional life. Meanwhile, Ruby finds the courage to take temporary custody of baby Harmony. When she hears the news, Violette agrees to try and get clean, and decides to send Arlo away. Elsewhere, Connie returns to the emergency department(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the third-round fixtures, including Manchester City v Port Vale at the Etihad Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester United at Molineux. Holders City hadn't faced the Valiants since October 1999, and were heavy favourites to progress against their League Two opponents. Wolves overcame United at the quarter-final stage last season, winning 2-1 on this ground thanks to second-half goals from Raul Jiminez and Diogo Jota. Plus, highlights of Rochdale or Boston United v Newcastle United, Bristol City v Shrewsbury Town, Millwall v Newport County, Rotherham United v Hull City, Burnley v Peterborough United, Birmingham City v Blackburn Rovers, Fulham v Aston Villa, Cardiff City v Forest Green Rovers or Carlisle United, Oxford United v Exeter City or Hartlepool United, Southampton v Huddersfield Town, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield Wednesday, Bristol Rovers or Plymouth Argyle v Coventry City or Ipswich Town, Reading v Blackpool, Watford v Tranmere(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a preview of this weekend's NFL Wildcard fixtures(n)

programme.title: Life on the Line (2015)
programme.desc: The head of a power line team is sent in to fix the grid just as a huge storm hits Texas. True-life action thriller, starring John Travolta, Kate Bosworth and Sharon Stone(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the third-round fixtures, including Manchester City v Port Vale at the Etihad Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester United at Molineux. Holders City hadn't faced the Valiants since October 1999, and were heavy favourites to progress against their League Two opponents. Wolves overcame United at the quarter-final stage last season, winning 2-1 on this ground thanks to second-half goals from Raul Jiminez and Diogo Jota. Plus, highlights of Rochdale or Boston United v Newcastle United, Bristol City v Shrewsbury Town, Millwall v Newport County, Rotherham United v Hull City, Burnley v Peterborough United, Birmingham City v Blackburn Rovers, Fulham v Aston Villa, Cardiff City v Forest Green Rovers or Carlisle United, Oxford United v Exeter City or Hartlepool United, Southampton v Huddersfield Town, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield Wednesday, Bristol Rovers or Plymouth Argyle v Coventry City or Ipswich Town, Reading v Blackpool, Watford v Tranmere(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A revisit to an impressive project at large Grade II listed building in Rochester, kent. The team also explore properties in Mountain Ash, South Wales, and Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lindy MacEwan thinks New Zealand could give her the fresh start she wants, but husband Steve has reservations, so they take their children down under for a trial week(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant offer their expert assistance to two teams scouring Kingston upon Thames seeking keenly priced items to take to auction in an effort to maximise their profits against each other. Host Charlie Ross observes their progress and also finds a piece of vintage tea advertising that might well be worth a packet(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is in Bristol to explore how Methodist Church founder John Wesley devised a service that still helps many Christians mark the new year(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who are journalists find a home in Devon with holiday let potential on a budget of £700,000. Away from the hunt, he meets a young sheep farmer(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of marine life in different environments around the north, beginning with a tropical coral reef which has medicinal properties for dolphins, and is used as a tool by tusk fish. In Japan, a shipwreck is home to the Asian sheepshead wrasse, which can change gender, while in the polar north, walruses struggle to find ice floes for their pups to rest on as climate change takes its toll on the environment(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Liverpool v Everton (Kick-off 4.01pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the third-round clash between the Merseyside rivals, which takes place at Anfield. These sides met on this ground in the Premier League last month, with the Reds prevailing 5-2 in what proved to be Marco Silva's final game in charge of the Toffees. The teams last played each other in the FA Cup at this same stage two years ago, and Jurgen Klopp's men also emerged victorious on that occasion, albeit by a closer 2-1 scoreline. Defender Virgil van Dijk scored the winner on his debut, following a £75million move from Southampton a matter of days beforehand(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A compilation programme championing cottage industries, with Ellie Harrison meeting a Herefordshire woman who sells hand-crafted products all revolving around fire(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. A terrifying plan to destroy humanity is about to reach fruition. Can the Doctor and her companions escape multiple traps and defeat a deadly alliance? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The return of the period drama about midwives and nurses in London's East End, picking up the story in January 1965. After dealing with a power cut at the maternity home, Fred follows an unusual sound and finds a newborn baby abandoned in a dustbin. Meanwhile, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane must deal with an alarming outbreak of diphtheria, and Sister Julienne receives news of an ambitious redevelopment programme that potentially threatens Nonnatus House. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Dr Stephen Ward reports the suspicious theft of some of his private photographs, and persistent rumours force Minister of War John Profumo into more denials and threats of legal action. Mandy Rice-Davies is enjoying her newfound press exposure, but the sordid tales of London's society sex parties and affairs with young models embarrass Christine's family. With Johnny's trial approaching, she considers running away from her problems(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the latest third-round fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield, Sheffield United v AFC Fylde at Bramall Lane and Middlesbrough v Tottenham Hotspur at Riverside Stadium. This was the second Merseyside derby at this stage in the last three seasons, while non-league Fylde aimed to produce one of the competition's great shocks against Chris Wilder's Premier League outfit, and Spurs boss Jose Mourinho began his bid to win the trophy he claimed with Chelsea in 2007(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at The Hive Stadium(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Thandi wants to uproot her family to New Zealand, but husband Theo isn't so sure. A trial week threatens to drive the two further apart. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Highlands, new entrant crofters Lynn and Sandra welcome more new sheep and take delivery of a batch of hens that they hope will lay the foundations of an 'egg empire'. In Northern Ireland, the Rodgers host a barbecue for the local Young Farmers Club, but it is no ordinary flame-grilled affair, while Raymond and Johnny gather the flock to wean this year's lambs in the Cairngorms(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three chefs recreate Monica's razor clams with a shallot, caper and tomato sauce, before the second group of three take on Marcus' calves liver with a Bordelaise sauce. In the next round, the six chefs aim to impress the judges with a dish of their own design, with just one hour and 15 minutes to prepare it(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Rio Conchos (1964)
programme.desc: A shipment of rifles is stolen by a former Confederate general in the aftermath of the American Civil War, and he begins selling them to hostile Apaches. A cavalry major is assigned to bring back the weapons, aided by a mismatched band including one of his fellow officers, a duplicitous Mexican and a drunken former rebel. Western, starring Richard Boone and Stuart Whitman(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits archive interviews with some of Britain's biggest stars of the 1960s, including Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Peter Sellers, Peter O'Toole and Julie Christie(n)

programme.title: South Pacific (1958)
programme.desc: A naval nurse falls in love with a French plantation owner on an American-held island during the Second World War. However, dark secrets surface from his past and result in her having to leave the colony and embark on a perilous mission with the help of a US Navy officer. Romantic musical based on the Rodgers and Hammerstein Broadway show, with Mitzi Gaynor, Rossano Brazzi, John Kerr and Ray Walston(n)

programme.title: Snow Chick: A Penguin's Tale
programme.desc: Documentary following the first few months in the life of an emperor penguin born in an Antarctic midwinter. Kate Winslet narrates as cameras follow the young bird, watching him learn to survive the dangers of predators and the treacherous landscape of his home, before he is thrown into the ocean for the very first time(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Benidorm stars Sherrie Hewson and Tony Maudsley seek fortune in Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire. Providing guidance are dealer Roo Irvine and auctioneer Tim Medhurst. They'll finish at auction in North Hykeham, but first music lover Tony joins a merry band of toy soldiers in Melton Mowbray. Meanwhile, food lover Sherrie learns about one of the country's oldest biscuits - Grantham Gingerbreads(n)

programme.title: Snow Cats and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan and Dr Lukarevsky keep a close eye on Koshka's family as they continue on the road to full recovery. They have to prove their ability to hunt and survive before they can be released back to the wild. Bryansk has become so domesticated it's a major challenge for Gordon and Victor to help him find his wild instincts. If he cannot he will not be able to be released and will have to spend the rest of his life in captivity(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts a match between the winners of series 12 and 13, with the Verbivores taking on the Escapologists in the test of knowledge and lateral thinking as they battle it out to be crowned Champion of Champions(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as two teams each made up of four distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the final of the festive edition of the academic quiz(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: A look across Hawaii's varied islands, discovering how they were made, and how its wildlife thrives. The most remote island chain on Earth, its tropical shores are hard to reach. However, for the hardy creatures that have made it there, such as the waterfall-climbing fish, carnivorous caterpillar and Laysan albatross, a land of opportunity awaits. From newly formed lava fields, to lush jungles, and vibrant coral reefs, these diverse and beautiful islands have it all. As this documentary reveals, its people are looking to the future and attempting to protect and foster its flora and fauna(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig calls for a bit of 'Quiet', this time inviting Andrew Maxwell, Sara Pascoe and Jimmy Carr to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: In Search of Dracula with Mark Gatiss
programme.desc: Mark Gatiss explores and celebrates the icon of popular culture. He begins at Orava Castle in Slovakia, as used in the classic vampire film Nosferatu, then heads to the London Library on the trail of Bram Stoker's newly discovered research literature. In Philadelphia, he studies Stoker's hand-written notes and examines the abandoned ideas, plotlines and characters that went on to become this world-famous story. Mark also meets with actors, experts and historians as he explores the Count's transition from page to screen - and even manages to reunite seven Hammer brides. Contributors include the latest Dracula, Danish actor Claes Bang(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows (2014)
programme.desc: A documentary crew follows the daily lives of four vampires sharing a house in New Zealand as they prepare for an annual gathering of the undead. A new member of the household introduces the bloodsuckers to modern life, but his failure to follow the laws of their kind brings his flatmates nothing but trouble. Horror comedy, directed by and starring Jemaine Clement and Taika Waititi(n)

programme.title: True Story (2015)
programme.desc: A disgraced New York Times reporter discovers that an accused killer has assumed his identity, and the resulting investigation turns into a game of cat and mouse. Fact-based drama, starring Jonah Hill, James Franco and Felicity Jones(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The time traveller returns for a fresh set of adventures. Intelligence agents around the world are under attack from alien forces. MI6 turns to the only people who can help - a certain Doctor and her companions. As they travel the globe for answers, attacks come from all sides, leaving Earth's security resting on the team's shoulders. But where will this planet-threatening conspiracy lead them? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star, with guest appearances from Stephen Fry and Lenny Henry(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show clips from the natural world, including an elephant seal that turned up on a zebra crossing in Rio de Janeiro and a moose stuck up a tree(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is in Florida to continue his search for the world's deadliest creatures. He goes swimming with a wild alligator, witnesses the hunting methods of the bottlenose dolphin and wades into a swamp in an attempt to catch the weird and wonderful amphiuma salamander. On dry land, he goes on the trail of the eastern diamondback rattlesnake, America's largest serpent, and joins scientists tagging wild bobcats(n)

programme.title: Supersized Earth
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell traces the story of how humans have transformed the world in just a single generation, visiting the largest and most ambitious engineering projects, exploring the power of human ingenuity and the making of the modern world. He begins by visiting some of the most amazing constructions on the planet, travelling from the glittering heights of the world's tallest building in Dubai to the bustling favelas of Rio and to the planet's biggest city of all - Tokyo. Along the way, he undertakes extraordinary feats, from dangling on a rope hundreds of metres above the ground to scuba-diving through Mexico City's toxic sewage(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the next stage of the contest, with robots Supernova, Frostbite, Wyrm, High 5, Crushtacean, Ironside 3, Pulsar and Apex battling it out through a series of nine face-offs. Professor Noel Sharkey, Dr Lucy Rogers and Professor Sethu Vijayakumar judge the outcomes, while Jonathan Pearce provides running commentary on the action(n)

programme.title: Alexander and the Terrible, Horrible, No Good, Very Bad Day (2014)
programme.desc: An 11-year-old boy has a day where everything seems to go wrong, but gets no sympathy from his family. He becomes convinced that bad things only ever happen to him, but then his parents, his brother and his sister all suffer terrible days of their own. Family comedy based on Judith Viorst's series of children's books, starring Ed Oxenbould, Steve Carell and Jennifer Garner(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Monty travels to Japan in spring to explore different aspects of the nation's gardens. Amidst the cherry blossoms, he begins his journey by visiting the earliest surviving boating garden of the Heian period. He also looks at the rolling green moss of a Buddhist garden and learns the secrets of creating a Zen landscape before visiting an unconventional garden created by a modern design legend. The presenter also learns about the art of Japanese stonemasonry, the famous tea ceremonies and receives a lesson in the delicate art of traditional flower arranging(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in St Albans - a converted farmyard barn with a modern twist, a zero-carbon self-build and a 1950s Scandinavian-style bungalow(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin explore four glamorous and beautifully designed homes in Florida. Properties include one of the largest homes to feature on the series, a waterfront plot in Miami's Indian Creek comprising two concrete block stacked at right angles and for sale at almost $30million. The duo also visit a self-build inspired by the modernists of the 1940s set in a lush jungle of a garden and a unique home comprising of three separate buildings raised on stilts(n)

programme.title: McFarland, USA (2015)
programme.desc: A former American football coach takes a job at an underprivileged school with a mostly Hispanic student body, and finds he has a lot to learn about their lives. When he discovers many of his pupils have a talent for running, he forms an athletics team and leads them to glory. Fact-based drama, starring Kevin Costner and Ramiro Rodriguez(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Croome, a Palladian mansion in Worcestershire, where experts Adam Partridge and David Fletcher find a hand-operated fire pump(n)

programme.title: Equator from the Air
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan explores habitats around the equator from the air, using helicopters, light aircraft, drones and satellite sensing, to examine the environmental challenges facing the animals that live in these environments. The globetrotting journey begins in Africa, where equatorial wildlife competes for space with a fast-growing human population, and features a satellite health-check on a humpback whale. The equipment is also used to monitor humans in the region, detecting illegal mining and watching the development of a refugee camp into a thriving community(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon challenges a rival regiment to a gruelling duel, involving map-reading, initiative tests, river-crossing and field-fighting, to prove they are fitter and better trained than any other troop. Vintage comedy with the bumbling Home Guard of Walmington-on-Sea, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Ian Lavender(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: David Harewood narrates a documentary exploring three of the most exotic and remote islands on the planet, beginning with the unique and extraordinary wildlife of Madagascar. As the oldest island on Earth, life has had time to evolve and there are now more unique plants and animals on Madagascar than any other, with footage of ring-tailed lemurs, labord's chameleons and Decken's sifakas(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Sydney
programme.desc: In this programme from 1991, the Aussie revisits his home town after 30 years away and finds it has flourished in his absence, turning into one of the most cosmopolitan cities in the world. He admires the new skyscrapers competing with the famous Harbour Bridge and Opera House on the city's skyline, samples the region's wines, and reminisces about his youth as a surf-hero(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from London
programme.desc: The Australian journalist and presenter assesses how the city has changed over the decades, with contributions from Michael Caine, Victoria Wood, Terence Donovan and Peter Cook. First broadcast in 1991(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: After migrating to Britain from Australia in the 1960s, Clive James established himself as a caustic and charismatic commentator on popular culture and a pioneer of television criticism. In his heyday he was one of the most famous faces on British TV, and secured access to some of the biggest stars of the day, including Mel Brooks, Jane Fonda and Frank Sinatra. He has gone on to prove himself a captivating memoirist - reflecting on a rich and eventful life - and also a major league poet and translator. Here, Mary Beard talks to the author, critic and broadcaster(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig continues airing questions on the theme of Quests, this time inviting Susan Calman, Joe Lycett and Holly Walsh to join regular panellist Alan Davies. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: For a Few Dollars More (1965)
programme.desc: An enigmatic gunslinger forms an uneasy alliance with a military veteran to track down a notorious outlaw - but tensions simmer as their very different motives for the hunt put them increasingly at odds. Sergio Leone's Western sequel, starring Clint Eastwood, Lee Van Cleef, Gian Maria Volonte, Josef Egger, Mara Krup and Klaus Kinski(n)

programme.title: Our Little Sister (2015)
programme.desc: Three sisters who share a house together receive word that their estranged father has died. They travel to attend his funeral, only to discover he has a teenage daughter by another mother, who is now an orphan. They invite the girl to live with them, sparking a journey of self-discovery for all four women. Drama based on Akimi Yoshida's comic-book series, starring Haruka Ayase, Masami Nagasawa, Kaho and Suzu Hirose. In Japanese(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke tackle a neglected and boring backyard in Birmingham for its owner Sophie, who wants the area full of birds, bees and butterflies(n)

programme.title: The Robe (1953)
programme.desc: A callous Roman centurion is assigned to oversee Christ's crucifixion, an experience he finds so moving that he decides to convert to the Christian faith in a bid to redeem himself. However, his new loyalties incur the wrath of the insane Emperor Caligula. Drama based on the novel by Lloyd C Douglas, starring Richard Burton, Jean Simmons, Victor Mature and Michael Rennie(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Steve Brown is in Somerset celebrating the work that volunteers and communities do in our countryside. He spends the day on the community farm that's bringing people together to learn about food, helping with mental health issues and doing its bit for the environment and sustainability. Steve also helps with a bee count, and meets the man who has turned his life around through woodworking(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary discovers the rich history of our nation's greatest stately homes through its food, beginning with Highclere Castle, the real-life Downton Abbey, renowned as the setting for some of the most lavish and influential weekend house parties in history. She takes to the estate kitchen, where she creates a delicious spread including raspberry tartlets and gamekeeper's stew, and an elaborate menu for a grand finale dinner(n)

programme.title: Wild Tales from the Village
programme.desc: Documentary following the wildlife near a remote French village, including red squirrels, wild boar, dormice and stone martens. As the animals struggle to survive the winter, they take full advantage of living so close to unsuspecting humans in their search for food, while trying not to be detected. Narrated by Tcheky Karyo(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: Part two of two. The horticultural enthusiast explores Japan during autumn. He begins as he did in the spring, at one of the 'three great gardens of Japan' to learn how they are protected from the coming winter. Monty follows the history of the Japanese garden through the military strongholds of past leaders to the many styles, new and old, of the iconic stroll garden and finds a slice of Victorian England in the heart of Tokyo. He also learns how the Japanese are weaving nature into their concrete urban jungle. He ends his journey by the Sea of Japan at a place that makes art out of its gardens(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Crowcombe Court, west Somerset, where experts James Lewis and Thomas Plant discover some promotional Victorian sausage plates(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes Baroness Tanni Grey-Thompson on a journey to Somerset to recreate a childhood holiday of 1980. The pair travel in a bright yellow Rover, just like her family car back in the day, and they visit the Cameley Lakes where the former Paralympian would have stayed. They also visit Longleat Safari park, go fishing, and visit the Wells Cathedral(n)

programme.title: Tomorrowland: A World Beyond (2015)
programme.desc: A boy genius in the 1960s finds his way into a spectacular futuristic metropolis while attempting to promote his idea for a jetpack. Decades later, he has grown into a disillusioned adult, while a girl who has the talent and enthusiasm he has lost also finds her way into this strange new world, and together they attempt to uncover its secrets. Fantasy adventure, starring George Clooney, Britt Robertson and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: The long-running show returns with action from the slalom event in Zagreb, Croatia. Plus, interviews with Mikaela American star Shiffrin and double amputee snowboarder Darren Swift, and a look at whether Dave Ryding can secure Great Britain's first-ever World Cup victory(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets off on a high-altitude trek from Kathmandu, capital of Nepal, to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, a secretive kingdom perched high on the Tibetan plateau, on the far side of the Himalayas. On a five-day expedition through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet, Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris battle huge river crossings, vast mudslides and dizzying mountain passes on a trip that would test even the hardiest new 4x4s to the limit. But Paddy, Freddie and Chris aren't crossing the Himalayas in new 4x4s. They're crossing the Himalayas in a small old Peugeot, a very compact old Renault, and Nepal's first (and only) home-built car(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell assess the profit-making potential of three more business ideas - a range of boxing technology, a coffee business that may be on shaky ground financially, and a female fashion advisor offering a new look to male clients. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: Geraint Thomas: The Road Will Decide
programme.desc: Documentary following the 2018 Tour de France winner as he battled to retain the title. The film captures the intensity of his journey as he negotiates 21 stages and 3,500km in 23 days, while facing extreme weather conditions, cancelled stages and competitors such as France's Julian Alaphilippe(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian tests his preconceptions of Albania, guided on a whistlestop tour of the country by local TV presenter and journalist Erjona Rusi. Along the way, he tries to discover why Albania has remained a stubbornly unfashionable holiday destination, meeting those at the extremes of the country, from the rural farmers to the billionaire playboys who live in the cities in the company of supermodels(n)

programme.title: Dara O Briain - Voice of Reason
programme.desc: An exclusive chance to see the comedian Dara O Briain's Voice of Reason live show from 2019, filmed at London's Eventim Apollo. In the show, Dara addresses problems with virtual reality, having too much technology at home, how to deal with missing members of the front row and how to cope with reports of your own demise(n)

programme.title: A Hard Day's Night (1964)
programme.desc: The Beatles, harassed by their manager and Paul's grandfather, embark on a train journey to London for a TV show, but find themselves dogged by all manner of problems, including the inevitable overexcited fans. Musical comedy, starring the Fab Four alongside Wilfrid Brambell, Victor Spinetti and Anna Quayle. Songs include Can't Buy Me Love, Don't Bother Me and the title track(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A musical compilation, featuring mature rockers Tina Turner and Phil Collins, all-conquering funki dredds Soul II Soul, dance queen Paula Abdul, northern soul girl Lisa Stansfield and the stubborn indie pop of the Beautiful South. Madchester favourites Stone Roses and Happy Mondays put in an appearance, along with Stock Aitken Waterman stalwarts Kylie Minogue, Jason Donovan and Sonia. Plus, Sydney Youngblood, Mike and the Mechanics, Rebel MC and Double Trouble, along with duets from strange bedfellows Marc Almond and Gene Pitney and Linda Ronstadt and Aaron Neville(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's last full year as British prime minister contrasted with global political change, while in the music world Pete Waterman's final year of chart domination heralded the dawn of Madchester. Some female artists brought attitude and power to the year, while an unlikely duet between a '60s legend and an '80s icon proved a charttopper and Jive Bunny kept the less discerning happy. As far as Top of the Pops was concerned, Radio 1's old guard was stood down as a team of fresh-faced recruits helmed the show. Contributors include Jason Donovan, Lisa Stansfield, Shaun Ryder, Chris Rea, Marc Almond, Sharleen Spiteri, Jazzie B and Jenny Powell(n)

programme.title: Soft Cell: Say Hello, Wave Goodbye
programme.desc: Cameras follow singer Marc Almond and musician Dave Ball as they reunite for an emotional, sold-out, farewell concert at London's O2 arena in September 2018, marking the 40th anniversary of Soft Cell, one of the most colourful and charismatic bands in the history of popular music. Footage follows the build-up to the gig and provides an intimate retrospective portrait of the duo at work, as well as revisiting places of their youth. Clips from the concert itself are woven in with period archive and music videos(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure, lead singer of Ultravox and one of the men behind Band Aid, and Kim Appleby, who had a string of hits with her sister Mel in the Stock, Aitken & Waterman-produced band Mel and Kim, revisit the 1980s to determine why certain cities produced their own diverse tunes. In the first episode, they explore London's New Romantic movement, travel to Coventry to investigate the rise of Ska and speak to some of Sheffield's electronic music pioneers(n)

programme.title: Depeche Mode at the BBC
programme.desc: A look back at past performances by the band, who emerged from the UK's post-punk scene over three decades ago. From their first appearance on Top of the Pops in 1981 to performing on Later with Jools Holland in 2009, this programme shares archive testimony and recent interviews from core members Dave Gahan, Vince Clarke, Martin Gore and Andy Fletcher. New Life, Just Can't Get Enough, Blasphemous Rumours and Personal Jesus are among some of the classic hits performed(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A musical compilation, featuring mature rockers Tina Turner and Phil Collins, all-conquering funki dredds Soul II Soul, dance queen Paula Abdul, northern soul girl Lisa Stansfield and the stubborn indie pop of the Beautiful South. Madchester favourites Stone Roses and Happy Mondays put in an appearance, along with Stock Aitken Waterman stalwarts Kylie Minogue, Jason Donovan and Sonia. Plus, Sydney Youngblood, Mike and the Mechanics, Rebel MC and Double Trouble, along with duets from strange bedfellows Marc Almond and Gene Pitney and Linda Ronstadt and Aaron Neville(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's last full year as British prime minister contrasted with global political change, while in the music world Pete Waterman's final year of chart domination heralded the dawn of Madchester. Some female artists brought attitude and power to the year, while an unlikely duet between a '60s legend and an '80s icon proved a charttopper and Jive Bunny kept the less discerning happy. As far as Top of the Pops was concerned, Radio 1's old guard was stood down as a team of fresh-faced recruits helmed the show. Contributors include Jason Donovan, Lisa Stansfield, Shaun Ryder, Chris Rea, Marc Almond, Sharleen Spiteri, Jazzie B and Jenny Powell(n)

programme.title: The Heroes of Telemark (1965)
programme.desc: German scientists in occupied Norway during the Second World War begin work on the manufacture of vital components for an atomic bomb, prompting the Norwegian resistance to team up with British forces on a campaign of sabotage to halt production. Second World War thriller, starring Kirk Douglas, Richard Harris, Anton Diffring, Roy Dotrice and Ulla Jacobsson(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: William and his team continue to search wells all over town for more clues, and realise that the killer could also have been operating across the border in Sweden, potentially multiplying the number of victims. Line's investigation is also becoming more complex, meaning Thomas has his work cut out trying to unite the family at Christmas. In Norwegian and English(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: Maggie pursues some unpopular, and not very Norwegian, investigative methods, while a discovery is made in the Larvik countryside. It is the breakthrough the case needed, but also the one they all feared. Line continues interviewing Viggo's old schoolmates and meets interesting local characters along the way. In Norwegian and English(n)

programme.title: Talking Comedy
programme.desc: A look back through the archives at Rowan Atkinson's appearances on some of the BBC's best-loved talk shows(n)

programme.title: TOTP2: 80s Special
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe presents a selection of memorable Top of the Pops performances from the 1980s. Featuring appearances by Adam and the Ants, Bucks Fizz, Soft Cell, Dexys Midnight Runners, Madness, Culture Club, Frankie Goes to Hollywood, Duran Duran, Wham!, Pet Shop Boys, the Communards, Whitney Houston, Bros, Tiffany, Yazz & the Plastic Population, Kylie and Jason, Black Box and Lisa Stansfield(n)

programme.title: Primal Scream: The Lost Memphis Tapes
programme.desc: The discovery of the band's missing tracks, laid down in Memphis' Ardent Studios in 1993, but subsequently mislaid. The band later re-recorded the songs with a new producer, and they were incorporated into their 1994 album Give Out But Don't Give Up, meaning the originals were left unreleased. With previously unreleased footage of behind-the-scenes studio sessions, this is the story of how the original mix tapes of the album were rediscovered in a basement by Andrew Innes, Primal Scream's rhythm guitarist(n)

programme.title: The R&B Feeling: The Bob Parks Story
programme.desc: Documentary offering an intimate portrait of the eccentric artist Bob Parks, from his time on the Los Angeles performance art scene of the early 1970s, through to his return to the UK, and his mutually dependent relationship with his ailing mother, Miggy. The film explores how the dissolution of his seemingly idyllic marriage to San Franciscan wife Myriam Morales saw Bob's burgeoning art career in the US fall to pieces. Spiralling into a self-destructive lifestyle, Bob was eventually 'rescued' by the parishioners of a South Central gospel church, who he joined in worship for the next six years. Eventually returning home to the New Forest for what was planned as a six-month stay, Bob takes up residence with his parents, and ends up staying for 30 years. The artist shares his love for gospel church worship, and explores what he calls 'the R&B feeling', all while trying to break free of his obsessive fixation on his mother - and her impending death(n)

programme.title: Essential Royal Ballet
programme.desc: Presented on location in Covent Garden at the Royal Opera House, Katie Derham weaves the history of ballet through excerpts from the past decade of performances. She also goes behind the scenes to see what it takes to be a dancer in The Royal Ballet as they prepare to take to the stage(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at The Hive Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Snow Queen
programme.desc: A performance by Scottish Ballet inspired by Hans Christian Andersen's fairytale and celebrating the company's 50th anniversary, recorded at Edinburgh's Festival Theatre(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate more potentially valuable works of art, beginning by helping a man who believes he owns a painting by French post-impressionist Edouard Vuillard. The quest for provenance begins in Geneva, where Philip compares the picture with a huge Vuillard work created in 1919, trying to find out whether the same materials were used in both, while Fiona uncovers clues to the picture's origins in France and Holland. Once they have enough evidence, they seek the approval of the Wildenstein Institute in Paris - but these are the same experts who notoriously rejected a highly credible Monet back in the very first episode. Have they done enough to prove this picture is genuine?(n)

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Looking for Margot
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn inspired generations of ballerinas, while her late-flowering partnership with Rudolf Nureyev created the most dazzling ballet partnership in history. However, her life was marked by tragedy and disappointment. Darcey finds that Margot couldn't find love and never had children, got drawn into a failed foreign revolution, danced on for too long, and died in poverty. The former ballerina goes behind the scenes at the Royal Opera House and the Royal Ballet, and travels from London to New York and Panama, discovering that Margot did eventually find a kind of happiness - though one that came at a high price(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Fara Dabhoiwala trawls five decades of the BBC archive to explore how documentary-makers have shifted their attitude toward explorers over the past five decades(n)

programme.title: Lucy Worsley: Elizabeth I's Battle for God's Music
programme.desc: The presenter investigates the story of choral evensong, which was instigated by Henry VIII during the tumultuous and violent era of the English Reformation. Lucy recounts how Elizabeth I and her two siblings were shaped by the changes their father brought about and how her ideas about religious music found their ultimate expression(n)

Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: With just 2 years' experience, Scottish teenager Iona has only five months to prepare for the biggest international competition of her life, the World Junior Surf Championships in the Azores, Portugal. It's a race against time as she trains in sub-zero conditions and battles the unpredictable seas of her home shores to find the perfect wave. It's punishing but day after day Iona dons her wetsuit and dives in. But surfing off Scotland's coast is a world away from the warm, volcanic waters of the Azores, where Iona will be up against the gnarliest (treacherous) waves she has ever faced as well as some of the most experienced and fearless surfers on the planet. Determined not to let herself or her country down, will Iona master the surf - and the pressure - to come home a champion?(n)

programme.desc: The Tombliboos' music makes everyone do things too fast. Igglepiggle and Upsy Daisy dance, while Makka Pakka washes stones - but when the music slows, so do they(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Jennie McAlpine reads tonight's bedtime story about a little girl and her bunny(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Oldbury, West Midlands, to see a town house with a purple theme, while Martel Maxwell visits a plot of land in Greater Manchester and a dated semi in Heslington, Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers offer their views on a backpack that can be twisted round to the front for easy access, a rotating wrist torch, and a button-making set that requires no glue, scissors or needles. With Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Margherita Taylor meets a couple who have a £550,000 budget and dream of swapping coastal life in Southend-on-Sea, Essex for views of rolling countryside. She also visits Westonbirt, the National Arboretum, where the annual arboricultural show is in full swing, and sees tree experts putting a range of impressive skills into action(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore obtains three items from a tip in Surrey. Creative duo Josh and Oli are masters of woodwork, but will they be left scratching their heads when Sarah drops off a pile of old floorboards? Screen printer Daniel Heath hopes his bold designs will make a wooden tea trolley desirable again, and Sarah falls in love with a retro box of camera-developing equipment(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a zen space for a young couple longing for a Japanese-style garden. Working to a £7,000 budget, the designers attempt to bring a taste of the Orient to Blackburn, turning a mismatched garden into an area of tranquillity and serenity(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Cadair Idris is a mountain steeped in legend and mythology and also one of Wales' most spectacular summits. Derek Brockway climbs three peaks in this range and enjoys a quick dip in its glacial lake. In the Brecon Beacons, it's a much more relaxing route as he meets up with singer and broadcaster Beverley Humphreys for a walk in the footsteps of one of history's most impressive sopranos - Madame Adelina Patti, exploring her former castle estate at Craig y Nos Country Park(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture restorer William Kirk takes on a cherished family rocking horse that is in desperate need of restoration. The paint work and plaster is flaking away and the leather work is tattered and torn, but saddler maker Suzie Fletcher is also on hand. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay works wonders on a Royal Doulton plaque, which was broken in two during a house move 57 years ago and has remained hidden in a drawer ever since. Plus, Brenton West works on a Victorian stereoscopic viewer. These were a popular form of entertainment for Victorians and make images appear in 3D for the viewer(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard feels the pressure as he designs and cooks a vegan spread for 160 wedding guests at a country estate house in south Wales. It's the bride and groom's special day and Matt must hit their exacting specifications, the standards of the venue and the expectations of the guests. With a cake and a selection of signature dishes to prepare, this tricky catering challenge has Matt in a spin(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: More than one family is torn apart following the events of Christmas Day and despite Walford's biggest secret being exposed, an even bigger one has now formed. Only a handful of people know the truth - and only time will tell whether it will remain hidden(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a Second World War drama, rarely performed because of its historical inaccuracy and poor research. Director Chris plays Rufus Heal, a dashing pilot reduced to a desk job cracking German codes in a top secret Allied facility after losing his leg. He is assisted by Sandra as uptight Englishwoman Valerie Sky and Vanessa as French codebreaker Camille, under the watchful eye of Annie as Wing Commander Wickham. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Having successfully made landfall in Whitby, Count Dracula heads for the gleaming lights of London where he finds a new environment pulsing with fresh blood. He lays his plans to spread his foul vampire contagion, focusing his sights on Lucy Westenra, a beautiful and popular girl with no shortage of young men in her thrall. However, he finds his plans unexpectedly checked by an alliance of formidable foes. Claes Bang, John Heffernan, Dolly Wells, Joanna Scanlan and Morfydd Clark star in the final instalment of Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss' adaptation of the Bram Stoker classic(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The day of Godmother and Dad's wedding arrives and while Jake wants to know where Claire is, the Priest manages to upstage the bride. As secrets are revealed and decisions are made, will Fleabag find the ending she is looking for? Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott, Sian Clifford, Bill Patterson and Olivia Colman(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Marking a decade since the sitcom first appeared on screen, the cast of the award-winning comedy series reunite in a special show at the London Palladium. A whole host of treats and surprises are lined up as they look back over a decade of fun, friendship, romance and all that made millions of fans connect with this unique and very special sitcom. With Miranda at the helm, they also share some of their fondest memories of the show, along with a series of behind-the-scenes tales(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Bryn Williams and Skye Gyngell, and special guest James May faces his food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard feels the pressure as he designs and cooks a vegan spread for 160 wedding guests at a country estate house in south Wales. It's the bride and groom's special day and Matt must hit their exacting specifications, the standards of the venue and the expectations of the guests. With a cake and a selection of signature dishes to prepare, this tricky catering challenge has Matt in a spin(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, as the FA Cup third round takes centre stage, with fixtures including Liverpool v Everton(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Richard Madley help two married couples find items to sell at auction in Ardingly, West Sussex, while Eric Knowles views artefacts relating to Lewis Carroll(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms four items saved from the tip in Manchester and Surrey with the help of upholsterer Anthony Devine, lighting expert Guy Trench and carpenter Norman Wilkinson(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Bolton couple Lorna and Luciano invite Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers to revamp their garden, adding entertainments for their two energetic sons and a classic Italian pizza oven. The owners have a generous budget of £6,500 to work within(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts a special edition celebrating 50 years of the quiz. She and regular team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson take a trip down memory lane as they are joined by three distinguished past guests in four-time Major-winning golfer Laura Davies, the most prolific try-scorer in English rugby league history Martin Offiah, and triple world champion gymnast Beth Tweddle, as well as former Rangers and Scotland striker Ally McCoist, who was once a team captain on the show(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the FA Cup, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: DC Miranda Blake is in Palma de Mallorca to escort criminal turned supergrass Niall Taylor back to the UK, but things don't go according to plan. She ends up being paired with wisecracking German detective Max Winter by local police chief Ines Villegas, who authorises the pair to look into Taylor's gang.Crime drama, starring Elen Rhys and Julian Looman(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host a children's television-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Paul Chuckle, Dani Harmer, Gwyneth Powell, George Christopher, Hacker, Dodge, Andy Day and Cerrie Burnell(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo return to host the second series of the dancing contest, with performers from a variety of styles competing to impress the viewing public and win a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing. Tonight, the competition kicks off with the auditions, and the performers need to impress the studio audience for the chance to progress to the next round. In a new twist, one dance captain will be able to pick an act and take them straight through to the live shows, and Love Island star and professional dancer Curtis Pritchard joins as the new receptionist, looking after all the acts backstage(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dylan decides it is time to take a stand, both in his personal and professional life. Meanwhile, Ruby finds the courage to take temporary custody of baby Harmony. When she hears the news, Violette agrees to try and get clean, and decides to send Arlo away. Elsewhere, Connie returns to the emergency department(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the third-round fixtures, including Manchester City v Port Vale at the Etihad Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester United at Molineux. Holders City hadn't faced the Valiants since October 1999, and were heavy favourites to progress against their League Two opponents. Wolves overcame United at the quarter-final stage last season, winning 2-1 on this ground thanks to second-half goals from Raul Jiminez and Diogo Jota. Plus, highlights of Rochdale or Boston United v Newcastle United, Bristol City v Shrewsbury Town, Millwall v Newport County, Rotherham United v Hull City, Burnley v Peterborough United, Birmingham City v Blackburn Rovers, Fulham v Aston Villa, Cardiff City v Forest Green Rovers or Carlisle United, Oxford United v Exeter City or Hartlepool United, Southampton v Huddersfield Town, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield Wednesday, Bristol Rovers or Plymouth Argyle v Coventry City or Ipswich Town, Reading v Blackpool, Watford v Tranmere(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a preview of this weekend's NFL Wildcard fixtures(n)

programme.title: Life on the Line (2015)
programme.desc: The head of a power line team is sent in to fix the grid just as a huge storm hits Texas. True-life action thriller, starring John Travolta, Kate Bosworth and Sharon Stone(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the third-round fixtures, including Manchester City v Port Vale at the Etihad Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester United at Molineux.
