programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A revisit to an impressive project at large Grade II listed building in Rochester, kent. The team also explore properties in Mountain Ash, South Wales, and Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Cheetah cubs have never been born at the park before, so the staff keep a close eye on first-time mum Wilma, as she copes with the new arrivals(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant offer their expert assistance to two teams scouring Kingston upon Thames seeking keenly priced items to take to auction in an effort to maximise their profits against each other. Host Charlie Ross observes their progress and also finds a piece of vintage tea advertising that might well be worth a packet(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: Return of the programme in which Kate and her Welsh sheepdog Teg explore Wales' countryside. She begins in North Wales, trying to climb the tallest man-made structure in the country to fix some loose cables on the Arfon Transmission Tower. Next, Kate learns how to overcome natural obstacles with the novel activity of packrafting - using a lightweight inflatable canoe. On the north-east coast, Kate meets a pack of dogs trained to carry out wildlife conservation by sniffing out endangered species, then on the north-west coast she digs and prepares a grave for a new resident who in death wants to give himself to help nature thrive(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is in Bristol to explore how Methodist Church founder John Wesley devised a service that still helps many Christians mark the new year(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who are journalists find a home in Devon with holiday let potential on a budget of £700,000. Away from the hunt, he meets a young sheep farmer(n)

programme.title: The 1900 Island
programme.desc: Four families with a longing to escape the demands of the modern world experience life in a fishing community as it was at the turn of the 20th century, heading to the small island of Llanddwyn off the coast of Anglesey. In the opening programme, the Powers, the Davies, the Barkers and couple Kate Evans and Arwel John all come together and realise they must adapt quickly - but bad weather, lack of experience and limited rations lead to hunger and frustrations. Can a last-minute effort by the women save the day?(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Liverpool v Everton (Kick-off 4.01pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the third-round clash between the Merseyside rivals, which takes place at Anfield. These sides met on this ground in the Premier League last month, with the Reds prevailing 5-2 in what proved to be Marco Silva's final game in charge of the Toffees. The teams last played each other in the FA Cup at this same stage two years ago, and Jurgen Klopp's men also emerged victorious on that occasion, albeit by a closer 2-1 scoreline. Defender Virgil van Dijk scored the winner on his debut, following a £75million move from Southampton a matter of days beforehand(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A compilation programme championing cottage industries, with Ellie Harrison meeting a Herefordshire woman who sells hand-crafted products all revolving around fire(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. A terrifying plan to destroy humanity is about to reach fruition. Can the Doctor and her companions escape multiple traps and defeat a deadly alliance? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The return of the period drama about midwives and nurses in London's East End, picking up the story in January 1965. After dealing with a power cut at the maternity home, Fred follows an unusual sound and finds a newborn baby abandoned in a dustbin. Meanwhile, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane must deal with an alarming outbreak of diphtheria, and Sister Julienne receives news of an ambitious redevelopment programme that potentially threatens Nonnatus House. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Dr Stephen Ward reports the suspicious theft of some of his private photographs, and persistent rumours force Minister of War John Profumo into more denials and threats of legal action. Mandy Rice-Davies is enjoying her newfound press exposure, but the sordid tales of London's society sex parties and affairs with young models embarrass Christine's family. With Johnny's trial approaching, she considers running away from her problems(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the latest third-round fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield, Sheffield United v AFC Fylde at Bramall Lane and Middlesbrough v Tottenham Hotspur at Riverside Stadium. This was the second Merseyside derby at this stage in the last three seasons, while non-league Fylde aimed to produce one of the competition's great shocks against Chris Wilder's Premier League outfit, and Spurs boss Jose Mourinho began his bid to win the trophy he claimed with Chelsea in 2007(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at The Hive Stadium(n)

There is also a new addition to the judging line-up as Michelin-starred chef Angela Hartnett joins home cooking queen Mary Berry and produce expert Chris Bavin. For their first two challenges, the cooks have to come up with their ultimate pasta dinner and a dish based on a piece of fruit(n)

programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo return to host the second series of the dancing contest, with performers from a variety of styles competing to impress the viewing public and win a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing. Tonight, the competition kicks off with the auditions, and the performers need to impress the studio audience for the chance to progress to the next round. In a new twist, one dance captain will be able to pick an act and take them straight through to the live shows, and Love Island star and professional dancer Curtis Pritchard joins as the new receptionist, looking after all the acts backstage(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dylan decides it is time to take a stand, both in his personal and professional life. Meanwhile, Ruby finds the courage to take temporary custody of baby Harmony. When she hears the news, Violette agrees to try and get clean, and decides to send Arlo away. Elsewhere, Connie returns to the emergency department(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the third-round fixtures, including Manchester City v Port Vale at the Etihad Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester United at Molineux. Holders City hadn't faced the Valiants since October 1999, and were heavy favourites to progress against their League Two opponents. Wolves overcame United at the quarter-final stage last season, winning 2-1 on this ground thanks to second-half goals from Raul Jiminez and Diogo Jota. Plus, highlights of Rochdale or Boston United v Newcastle United, Bristol City v Shrewsbury Town, Millwall v Newport County, Rotherham United v Hull City, Burnley v Peterborough United, Birmingham City v Blackburn Rovers, Fulham v Aston Villa, Cardiff City v Forest Green Rovers or Carlisle United, Oxford United v Exeter City or Hartlepool United, Southampton v Huddersfield Town, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield Wednesday, Bristol Rovers or Plymouth Argyle v Coventry City or Ipswich Town, Reading v Blackpool, Watford v Tranmere(n)

programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a preview of this weekend's NFL Wildcard fixtures(n)

programme.title: Life on the Line (2015)
programme.desc: The head of a power line team is sent in to fix the grid just as a huge storm hits Texas. True-life action thriller, starring John Travolta, Kate Bosworth and Sharon Stone(n)

programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A revisit to an impressive project at large Grade II listed building in Rochester, kent. The team also explore properties in Mountain Ash, South Wales, and Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lindy MacEwan thinks New Zealand could give her the fresh start she wants, but husband Steve has reservations, so they take their children down under for a trial week(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant offer their expert assistance to two teams scouring Kingston upon Thames seeking keenly priced items to take to auction in an effort to maximise their profits against each other. Host Charlie Ross observes their progress and also finds a piece of vintage tea advertising that might well be worth a packet(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is in Bristol to explore how Methodist Church founder John Wesley devised a service that still helps many Christians mark the new year(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who are journalists find a home in Devon with holiday let potential on a budget of £700,000. Away from the hunt, he meets a young sheep farmer(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of marine life in different environments around the north, beginning with a tropical coral reef which has medicinal properties for dolphins, and is used as a tool by tusk fish. In Japan, a shipwreck is home to the Asian sheepshead wrasse, which can change gender, while in the polar north, walruses struggle to find ice floes for their pups to rest on as climate change takes its toll on the environment(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Liverpool v Everton (Kick-off 4.01pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the third-round clash between the Merseyside rivals, which takes place at Anfield. These sides met on this ground in the Premier League last month, with the Reds prevailing 5-2 in what proved to be Marco Silva's final game in charge of the Toffees. The teams last played each other in the FA Cup at this same stage two years ago, and Jurgen Klopp's men also emerged victorious on that occasion, albeit by a closer 2-1 scoreline. Defender Virgil van Dijk scored the winner on his debut, following a £75million move from Southampton a matter of days beforehand(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A compilation programme championing cottage industries, with Ellie Harrison meeting a Herefordshire woman who sells hand-crafted products all revolving around fire(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. A terrifying plan to destroy humanity is about to reach fruition. Can the Doctor and her companions escape multiple traps and defeat a deadly alliance? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The return of the period drama about midwives and nurses in London's East End, picking up the story in January 1965. After dealing with a power cut at the maternity home, Fred follows an unusual sound and finds a newborn baby abandoned in a dustbin. Meanwhile, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane must deal with an alarming outbreak of diphtheria, and Sister Julienne receives news of an ambitious redevelopment programme that potentially threatens Nonnatus House. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Dr Stephen Ward reports the suspicious theft of some of his private photographs, and persistent rumours force Minister of War John Profumo into more denials and threats of legal action. Mandy Rice-Davies is enjoying her newfound press exposure, but the sordid tales of London's society sex parties and affairs with young models embarrass Christine's family. With Johnny's trial approaching, she considers running away from her problems(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the latest third-round fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield, Sheffield United v AFC Fylde at Bramall Lane and Middlesbrough v Tottenham Hotspur at Riverside Stadium. This was the second Merseyside derby at this stage in the last three seasons, while non-league Fylde aimed to produce one of the competition's great shocks against Chris Wilder's Premier League outfit, and Spurs boss Jose Mourinho began his bid to win the trophy he claimed with Chelsea in 2007(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at The Hive Stadium(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Oldbury, West Midlands, to see a town house with a purple theme, while Martel Maxwell visits a plot of land in Greater Manchester and a dated semi in Heslington, Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers offer their views on a backpack that can be twisted round to the front for easy access, a rotating wrist torch, and a button-making set that requires no glue, scissors or needles. With Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Margherita Taylor meets a couple who have a £550,000 budget and dream of swapping coastal life in Southend-on-Sea, Essex for views of rolling countryside. She also visits Westonbirt, the National Arboretum, where the annual arboricultural show is in full swing, and sees tree experts putting a range of impressive skills into action(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore obtains three items from a tip in Surrey. Creative duo Josh and Oli are masters of woodwork, but will they be left scratching their heads when Sarah drops off a pile of old floorboards? Screen printer Daniel Heath hopes his bold designs will make a wooden tea trolley desirable again, and Sarah falls in love with a retro box of camera-developing equipment(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich use some unusual items to makeover gardens in Hoveringham and Coventry, with half an old boat and a tin air-raid shelter being transformed(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture restorer William Kirk takes on a cherished family rocking horse that is in desperate need of restoration. The paint work and plaster is flaking away and the leather work is tattered and torn, but saddler maker Suzie Fletcher is also on hand. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay works wonders on a Royal Doulton plaque, which was broken in two during a house move 57 years ago and has remained hidden in a drawer ever since. Plus, Brenton West works on a Victorian stereoscopic viewer. These were a popular form of entertainment for Victorians and make images appear in 3D for the viewer(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts a special edition celebrating 50 years of the quiz. She and regular team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson take a trip down memory lane as they are joined by three distinguished past guests in four-time Major-winning golfer Laura Davies, the most prolific try-scorer in English rugby league history Martin Offiah, and triple world champion gymnast Beth Tweddle, as well as former Rangers and Scotland striker Ally McCoist, who was once a team captain on the show(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: More than one family is torn apart following the events of Christmas Day and despite Walford's biggest secret being exposed, an even bigger one has now formed. Only a handful of people know the truth - and only time will tell whether it will remain hidden(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a Second World War drama, rarely performed because of its historical inaccuracy and poor research. Director Chris plays Rufus Heal, a dashing pilot reduced to a desk job cracking German codes in a top secret Allied facility after losing his leg. He is assisted by Sandra as uptight Englishwoman Valerie Sky and Vanessa as French codebreaker Camille, under the watchful eye of Annie as Wing Commander Wickham. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Having successfully made landfall in Whitby, Count Dracula heads for the gleaming lights of London where he finds a new environment pulsing with fresh blood. He lays his plans to spread his foul vampire contagion, focusing his sights on Lucy Westenra, a beautiful and popular girl with no shortage of young men in her thrall. However, he finds his plans unexpectedly checked by an alliance of formidable foes. Claes Bang, John Heffernan, Dolly Wells, Joanna Scanlan and Morfydd Clark star in the final instalment of Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss' adaptation of the Bram Stoker classic(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The day of Godmother and Dad's wedding arrives and while Jake wants to know where Claire is, the Priest manages to upstage the bride. As secrets are revealed and decisions are made, will Fleabag find the ending she is looking for? Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott, Sian Clifford, Bill Patterson and Olivia Colman(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Marking a decade since the sitcom first appeared on screen, the cast of the award-winning comedy series reunite in a special show at the London Palladium. A whole host of treats and surprises are lined up as they look back over a decade of fun, friendship, romance and all that made millions of fans connect with this unique and very special sitcom. With Miranda at the helm, they also share some of their fondest memories of the show, along with a series of behind-the-scenes tales(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Bryn Williams and Skye Gyngell, and special guest James May faces his food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi-detached house in Folkestone in Kent, and a terraced property in Hanley, Stoke-on-Trent, are sold at auction(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, as the FA Cup third round takes centre stage, with fixtures including Liverpool v Everton(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Richard Madley help two married couples find items to sell at auction in Ardingly, West Sussex, while Eric Knowles views artefacts relating to Lewis Carroll(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms four items saved from the tip in Manchester and Surrey with the help of upholsterer Anthony Devine, lighting expert Guy Trench and carpenter Norman Wilkinson(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the return of the competition in which 10 home cooks move into a shared house and compete in a series of weekly challenges designed to demonstrate their flair, skill and creativity. There is also a new addition to the judging line-up as Michelin-starred chef Angela Hartnett joins home cooking queen Mary Berry and produce expert Chris Bavin. For their first two challenges, the cooks have to come up with their ultimate pasta dinner and a dish based on a piece of fruit(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: DC Miranda Blake is in Palma de Mallorca to escort criminal turned supergrass Niall Taylor back to the UK, but things don't go according to plan. programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at The Hive Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues that judges - especially those of the European Court of Human Rights - have usurped power by expanding the interpretation of human rights law. He explains that concepts of human rights have a long history in the common law. But by contrast, the European Convention on Human Rights has become a dynamic treaty, taking on new interpretations and powers(n)

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the House of Lords of a peer thanking the Queen for delivering the Queen's Speech in parliament, from Thursday 19 December(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Yeoman Warder Christopher Skaife talks to Mark D'Arcy about his new book 'The Ravenmaster' on life in the Tower of London.(n)

programme.title: Reflections: When Parties Split
programme.desc: Series of half-hour unscripted talks on party splits(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: The review of the day's events in Westminster(n)

programme.title: Mayor's Question Time
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the London Assembly of questions to the Mayor of London, Sadiq Khan(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to historian Lauren Johnson about her new book Shadow King: The Life and Death of Henry VI(n)

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: BBC Wales journalist Elliw Gwawr visits Highgate Cottage in Llanystumdwy, the childhood home of First World War Premier David Lloyd George(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: The speech and question session with former prime minister Tony Blair on the future for Labour and progressive politics, from Wednesday 18 December(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to former supreme court justice Jonathan Sumption about his new book 'Trials of the State: Law and the Decline of Politics'(n)

programme.title: Reflections: When Parties Split
programme.desc: In the final programme in a series of half-hour unscripted talks on party splits, political journalist Steve Richards looks at how Brexit has split the two main political parties(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: A panel of scholars debate the legacy of the Treaty of Versailles at an event at the LSE, with professors Michael Cox, David Stevenson, Linda Yueh and Barry Buzan, from Thursday 28 November.(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to politics professors Tim Bale and Paul Webb about their new book 'Footsoldiers' on political party membership.(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues against Britain adopting a written constitution as a response to political alienation, suggesting that it would be better change the system of voting. Presented and chaired by Anita Anand(n)

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Party historian Lord Lexden visits Astley Hall, home to the Conservative leader who was three times Prime Minister(n)

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: The first Duke of Wellington - victor at the Battle of Waterloo - was a military hero who served twice as Prime Minister. Carolyn Quinn meets the current Duke for a tour of the family's London home, Apsley House(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: He is one of the most controversial figures to have sat in the Speaker's chair in the House of Commons. As John Bercow bows out after ten controversial years as Speaker, BBC Parliament revisits the career of a modernising but polarising figure. See the teenage Bercow's ‘Question Time' debut, not from the Commons but on the TV programme. Relive the former Tory MP's clashes with ministers as he's accused of bias and of bending the rules to try to stop Brexit. In addition - with more Commons archive - Bercow Bows Out offers the chance to re-live some of his favourite unique phrases, whether he's advising an MP to search for ‘a soothing medicament' or accusing others of ‘chuntering from a sedentary position'(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption was until recently a justice of the UK's Supreme Court, as well as being a distinguished historian. He argues that the law is taking over the space once occupied by politics. Presented and chaired by Anita Anand(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

Ray calls at the garage and is impressed to discover Abi's a mechanic, until he finds out she's also an ex-con(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Setting off from Rosthwaite, a small village in the jaws of Borrowdale, the expert walker takes in the history and landscape of the Cumbrian fells as she heads up the small peak Castle Crag, which stands at less than 1,000ft. The presenter is joined by celebrated mountaineer Chris Bonington, to whom she asks - which is better - Everest or Castle Crag? From the lush green valley floor to magnificent views over Derwent Water, this walk is a great way to take home a lasting memory of the Lake District, and all in under three hours(n)

In the first episode, Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Ken Jeong make up the panel, trying to identify the performers inside a bee, a duck, a unicorn, a hedgehog, a butterfly and a chameleon(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as TV presenters Donna Air, Amanda Lamb, Sarah Greene and Dan Snow pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Following on from the launch show over Christmas, Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby host the start of the celebrity skating challenge, joined by judges Christopher Dean, Jayne Torvill, Ashley Banjo and new addition John 'Fabulous' Barrowman. This year's celebrities - six of whom will perform tonight - are Ben Hanlin, Caprice, Joe Swash, Kevin Kilbane, Libby Clegg, Lisa George, Lucrezia Millarini, Maura Higgins, Perri Kiely and Trisha Goddard and - in the show's first same-sex partnership - Ian 'H' Watkins. Michael Barrymore was due to compete, but a broken wrist has ruled him out, and he is replaced by Radzi Chinyanganya(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Another six unknown celebrities battle it out in the singing contest that has taken other countries by storm. The mystery contestants are dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes designed to hide their identities, while the panel - Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Ken Jeong - tries to guess who they are. By the end of the show, the second celebrity's identity will be revealed and they will be waving goodbye to the show. Joel Dommett hosts the proceedings(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Actress-writer Ruth Jones, comedian and actor Johnny Vegas and boxing star David Haye take their turns at answering the 15 questions that could bag them £1million for their chosen charities. programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Setting off from Rosthwaite, a small village in the jaws of Borrowdale, the expert walker takes in the history and landscape of the Cumbrian fells as she heads up the small peak Castle Crag, which stands at less than 1,000ft. The presenter is joined by celebrated mountaineer Chris Bonington, to whom she asks - which is better - Everest or Castle Crag? From the lush green valley floor to magnificent views over Derwent Water, this walk is a great way to take home a lasting memory of the Lake District, and all in under three hours(n)

programme.desc: Steve and his friends start up a live-action role-playing game, and Francine finds herself strangely drawn to the fantasy world they create. Meanwhile, Stan opens a marine animal theme park in the Smith family home(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Juice
programme.desc: Keith Lemon, Holly Willoughby and Fearne Cotton embark on an end-of-series cruise, but when the captain - Towie's Arg - sails the ship straight into a Bermuda Triangle-style vortex, they end up stranded on a desert island. That's when they start reminiscing about series 13 and 14 of the near-the-knuckle game show(n)

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: Horror game show, hosted by Reggie Yates, in which three gymnastics teachers enter a forest at dusk and endure a series of spooky challenges during which they must unlock chests full of prize money. In order to win the jackpot they need to get back out again, but the cash is protected by a pack of angry dogs(n)

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates a selection of peculiar record-breaking attempts, including the world's longest sausage chain, a couple with a record-breaking number of tattoos and the farthest distance skateboarding by a goat(n)

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a fresh selection of surreal mishaps filmed by viewers on their webcams, mobile phones and camcorders, featuring a range of hilarious footage(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in the ultimate shopping challenge, as they try to win the affections of footballer Roger by each buying him an outfit(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, drama student Josiah, factory worker Daniel and support worker Tomas hope to win a date with make-up artist Chanel. All they have to do is find her the perfect outfit - limited only by time, budget and their own sense of style(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: n London, model Elesha is hoping to be impressed by the style choices of three potential suitors. To impress her, they have to dress her, as Elesha has no other information about them but the outfits they buy. At the end of the challenge, which of the three competitors will she choose to take out on a date?(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the singing contest in which a celebrity panel and a studio audience must guess the famous faces performing behind elaborate costumes. In the first episode, Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Ken Jeong make up the panel, trying to identify the performers inside a bee, a duck, a unicorn, a hedgehog, a butterfly and a chameleon(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates a selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile-phone footage from the You've Been Framed! archives featuring children, from messy eaters to troublesome teens and sibling rivalries(n)

programme.title: Spy Kids 4: All the Time in the World (2011)
programme.desc: A secret agent decides to retire after having a baby, while her two stepchildren remain completely unaware of her exciting past. They stumble on to the truth when she is recalled to duty and, with the aid of two former junior spies, they help battle an evil mastermind who has the power to control time. Family adventure, starring Jessica Alba, Jeremy Piven, Rowan Blanchard and Mason Cook(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl (2003)
programme.desc: A buccaneer and a blacksmith commandeer a British ship and set sail in pursuit of a pirate captain who has kidnapped a colonial governor's daughter. However, their quarry and his crew are under a curse that turns them into living skeletons - and makes them impossible to kill. Swashbuckling fantasy adventure, starring Johnny Depp, Orlando Bloom, Keira Knightley, Geoffrey Rush, Jack Davenport and Jonathan Pryce(n)

programme.title: Skyfall (2012)
programme.desc: James Bond pursues a mysterious criminal mastermind who has orchestrated a terrorist attack on MI6 headquarters and exposed the identities of undercover agents around the world. The trail leads to a rogue former operative who has a personal vendetta against the intelligence organisation's leader M - who is also facing calls to retire. Sam Mendes directs the 23rd instalment in the spy franchise, starring Daniel Craig as 007, with Javier Bardem, Judi Dench, Ralph Fiennes, Naomie Harris and Ben Whishaw(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg becomes deeply religious and tries to convert Brian from atheism. Meanwhile, Stewie kidnaps all the cast members of Star Trek: The Next Generation and forces them to be his friends(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is proud when Jack's story appears in a national paper. Liz worries about Steve's whereabouts. Bet tries to forget Charlie by going on a shopping spree(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre books a holiday in Morocco to get away from the Street. Des cannot bring himself to tell Raquel about Tanya. Curly is forced into letting Steve stay at number seven(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The embers of a romance turn into flames for Pollard. Chris's pride comes before a fall. Jessica's news alarms Biff(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Michael's behaviour worries Rachel, while Viv's sister brings a warning. Zoe decides to give Chris a chance. Jessica is deeply unimpressed by Bernard's invitation(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local man confesses to a girl's murder of 11 years previously, prompting Sgt Miller to reopen the case. A search fails to uncover the victim's body, but the investigation takes an intriguing twist when PC Younger unearths a male skeleton instead. Meanwhile, Peggy sets her sights on a wealthy widower. Rural drama, guest starring Graham Turner and Geoffrey Hutchings, with John Duttine, Steven Blakeley and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp (1980)
programme.desc: Seedy landlord Rigsby takes in a seemingly respectable new lodger who jeopardises his efforts to romance tenant Miss Jones - but the new arrival may not be all he appears. Meanwhile, the addition of a rugby playing art student to the house sparks a fitness craze that leads Rigsby to enter a boxing match. Comedy based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A group of young offenders come to stay at Ashfordly Hall, but soon find themselves under suspicion after a spate of disturbances in the village. Meanwhile, Blaketon and Ventress go on the hunt for a spinster's missing dog, and a £50 reward gives Peggy an idea. Rural drama, starring Rupert Vansittart, William Simons, Derek Fowlds and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv has some unwelcome news for Vic. The Sugdens name the day and Bernard has plans for the village's future. Betty lays the law down to Seth. Jessica has to face up to her future(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic runs into difficulties on the moors. Biff shows his true colours. Lynn makes Jack an offer, while Seth's new living arrangements cause speculation(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack tries to hide the paper from Vera when news of his affair breaks. Maud tells Percy about Maureen's real father. Deirdre leaves for Morocco(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily supports Percy over finishing with Maud. The Websters decide to have a holiday in Blackpool. Jack's attempts to cook a nice meal for Vera backfire spectacularly(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A group of young offenders come to stay at Ashfordly Hall, but soon find themselves under suspicion after a spate of disturbances in the village. Meanwhile, Blaketon and Ventress go on the hunt for a spinster's missing dog, and a £50 reward gives Peggy an idea. Rural drama, starring Rupert Vansittart, William Simons, Derek Fowlds and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun
programme.desc: Drama about the discovery of the Egyptian Pharaoh's tomb. Archaeologist Howard Carter is recruited in 1905 by aristocrat and amateur Egyptologist Lord Carnarvon to explore the Valley of the Kings, and discovers evidence of the boy-king's final resting place. However, his expedition is threatened by global events and his own impulsive nature. Starring Max Irons and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Mutiny on the Buses (1972)
programme.desc: Chirpy bus driver Stan Butler lands himself in trouble as he struggles to raise enough money to marry his girlfriend, and on top of training his unemployed brother-in-law for a job at the depot he has to contend with an officious new manager. Comedy based on the sitcom, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis, Bob Grant, Michael Robbins, Anna Karen and Doris Hare(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans steps into the shoes of John Thaw in this 1965-set prequel to Inspector Morse, playing a younger version of the cerebral detective involved in the hunt for a missing schoolgirl. Soon deep in a full-blown murder investigation, Endeavour is sidelined, discredited and at a dead end - so he risks all to begin his own quest for justice. Roger Allam and Charlie Creed-Miles co-star in the mystery(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Medical drama about a junior doctor who becomes disillusioned with her life and decides to leave the UK. She heads for South India hoping to make a fresh start, and finds a new home working in an under-resourced and over-worked cottage hospital run by an eccentric English ex-pat. Medical drama, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby gets to grips with her challenging new routine working at the under-resourced and overworked hospital, but tension mounts when she begins to question Gabriel's professional conduct. The doctors have a troubling mystery on their hands when an unidentified man is discovered washed up on the beach, and also face a race against the clock to find a local snake catcher whose luck may have run out, leaving him in urgent need of treatment. Medical drama set in India, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell come to the aid of Christine Gillard, who as a teenager parted ways with her two-year-old daughter Marguerite, and has lived with the pain of their separation for nearly 50 years. The youngster was placed with a long-term foster family while her mother struggled to make ends meet, and four years later, Christine's circumstances meant she felt she had to turn down a chance for a reunion. The pair also try to help 41-year-old father-of-three Robert Lindsay, who was raised by his mother and grandparents in Kilmarnock, and now hopes to reconnect with his estranged Croatian dad(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: After resigning from his job as a traffic warden, henpecked George wonders how he can break the news to wife Mildred without incurring her wrath. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Henpecked George finally finds a job, but the line of work could make him unpopular with road users. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. As DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new forensic pathologist Kam Karimore investigate the mysterious crime, they find themselves drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching during their quest to identify the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Helen Baxendale and Navin Chowdhry(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When a respectable GP is found dead in a public lavatory, the young Morse's outlandish theories see him relegated to general duties by the newly arrived Chief Superintendent Bright. Humiliated and humbled, Endeavour has to investigate from the sidelines, but it's not long before he's questioning DI Fred Thursday's orders and risking his career to avert a family tragedy. Shaun Evans stars, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The local gossips go to town when a naked and mutilated corpse is discovered in the middle of a crop circle. However, their lurid tales of extraterrestrial abduction soon take a turn for the macabre when more similarly injured cadavers are discovered, causing DCI Barnaby to question his down-to-earth approach. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles(n)

Comedy based on the sitcom, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis, Bob Grant, Michael Robbins, Anna Karen and Doris Hare(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans steps into the shoes of John Thaw in this 1965-set prequel to Inspector Morse, playing a younger version of the cerebral detective involved in the hunt for a missing schoolgirl. Soon deep in a full-blown murder investigation, Endeavour is sidelined, discredited and at a dead end - so he risks all to begin his own quest for justice. Roger Allam and Charlie Creed-Miles co-star in the mystery(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Medical drama about a junior doctor who becomes disillusioned with her life and decides to leave the UK. She heads for South India hoping to make a fresh start, and finds a new home working in an under-resourced and over-worked cottage hospital run by an eccentric English ex-pat. Medical drama, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby gets to grips with her challenging new routine working at the under-resourced and overworked hospital, but tension mounts when she begins to question Gabriel's professional conduct. The doctors have a troubling mystery on their hands when an unidentified man is discovered washed up on the beach, and also face a race against the clock to find a local snake catcher whose luck may have run out, leaving him in urgent need of treatment. Medical drama set in India, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell come to the aid of Christine Gillard, who as a teenager parted ways with her two-year-old daughter Marguerite, and has lived with the pain of their separation for nearly 50 years. The youngster was placed with a long-term foster family while her mother struggled to make ends meet, and four years later, Christine's circumstances meant she felt she had to turn down a chance for a reunion. The pair also try to help 41-year-old father-of-three Robert Lindsay, who was raised by his mother and grandparents in Kilmarnock, and now hopes to reconnect with his estranged Croatian dad(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: After resigning from his job as a traffic warden, henpecked George wonders how he can break the news to wife Mildred without incurring her wrath. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. As DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new forensic pathologist Kam Karimore investigate the mysterious crime, they find themselves drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching during their quest to identify the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Helen Baxendale and Navin Chowdhry(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When a respectable GP is found dead in a public lavatory, the young Morse's outlandish theories see him relegated to general duties by the newly arrived Chief Superintendent Bright. Humiliated and humbled, Endeavour has to investigate from the sidelines, but it's not long before he's questioning DI Fred Thursday's orders and risking his career to avert a family tragedy. Shaun Evans stars, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The local gossips go to town when a naked and mutilated corpse is discovered in the middle of a crop circle. However, their lurid tales of extraterrestrial abduction soon take a turn for the macabre when more similarly injured cadavers are discovered, causing DCI Barnaby to question his down-to-earth approach. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles(n)

programme.desc: A profile of the former Manchester United and England player(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The sleuthing pathologist meets a wall of silence from medical chiefs who refuse to acknowledge that a doctor's blunder while performing an abortion caused the death of a patient(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo makes a discovery at a murder scene that makes him realise his nephew is on drugs. He turns to a former addict for support in putting the young man through cold turkey(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Bohemia, as Holmes is outwitted by a bewitching woman's blackmail who owns a compromising photograph of a European royal. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett as the brilliant Baker Street sleuth, with David Burke as his trusty companion Dr Watson, guest starring Gayle Hunnicutt(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur dabbles in the world of nouvelle cuisine, taking bookings on banquets. However, his chef goes on the run from a group of gangsters leaving him in the lurch when catering for a policeman's retirement party. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A visiting American senator is assassinated in broad daylight. After a newspaper publishes the names of witnesses, Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect them, but the task soon gets out of hand. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A villain comes out of hiding in an attempt to retrieve stolen loot and only Regan and Carter can stop him. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The pathologist's new girlfriend, Lynne, asks him to marry her, but pressure of work and memories of his troubled relationship with first wife Helen prevent him accepting(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Two brothers end up killing a priest at a Catholic school when a truck hijack goes wrong. The case proves embarrassing for the cops, since Crocker was giving a lecture at the time(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Strange, seemingly malevolent encoded messages sour a wealthy man's wedded bliss, prompting him to seek Holmes's expertise. Victorian mystery, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Boxer Lewis Nelson hires Ray to keep his gambling-addicted wife away from the casino he partly owns. However, she persuades Arthur to play the roulette wheels on her behalf, tempting him with the 'winning system' she has devised. Comedy starring George Cole(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Cowley and the team present a line of defence against groups of mercenaries and guerrillas who are waging war on the streets of Britain. David Suchet and Geoffrey Palmer guest star alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is called in to catch a gunman who has stolen cash from a lock-up. Hard-hitting detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four celebrity contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Emmerdale's Michael Parr (Ross Barton), England footballer Alex Scott, Hollyoaks actress Jennifer Metcalfe (Mercedes McQueen) and TV presenter Bill Turnbull must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade continues his investigations into supposedly deadly freshwater creatures. From right across the Amazon there are stories of a colossal fish that is said to swallow men whole. Some call it the 'lau-lau', others the 'piraiba'. But after 20 years of criss-crossing South America, Jeremy has never seen a specimen big enough to convince him that the fishermen's tales are true. In this programme, he heads to a remote and little-known backwater of Guyana on a mission to finally track down this alleged monster(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Hundreds died when the Sobral Santos II capsized not far from the shores of the Amazon in September 1981, and many of the stricken passengers were reportedly dragged to their deaths by powerful unseen attackers. Freshwater detective Jeremy Wade attempts to identify the culprits that played a part in the river's worst-ever boating disaster, but has the trail gone cold after more than 30 years?(n)

programme.title: Jaws 3 (1983)
programme.desc: A shark is captured by divers and put on display in a Florida marine centre, where it ultimately dies. However, examination of the body reveals the seemingly fully-grown shark was actually a baby - and its enormous, ferocious mother soon comes looking for it. Thriller sequel, originally released in cinemas in 3D, starring Dennis Quaid, Simon MacCorkindale, Louis Gossett Jr and Bess Armstrong(n)

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four celebrity contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Emmerdale's Michael Parr (Ross Barton), England footballer Alex Scott, Hollyoaks actress Jennifer Metcalfe (Mercedes McQueen) and TV presenter Bill Turnbull must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade continues his investigations into supposedly deadly freshwater creatures. From right across the Amazon there are stories of a colossal fish that is said to swallow men whole. Some call it the 'lau-lau', others the 'piraiba'. But after 20 years of criss-crossing South America, Jeremy has never seen a specimen big enough to convince him that the fishermen's tales are true. In this programme, he heads to a remote and little-known backwater of Guyana on a mission to finally track down this alleged monster(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Hundreds died when the Sobral Santos II capsized not far from the shores of the Amazon in September 1981, and many of the stricken passengers were reportedly dragged to their deaths by powerful unseen attackers. Freshwater detective Jeremy Wade attempts to identify the culprits that played a part in the river's worst-ever boating disaster, but has the trail gone cold after more than 30 years?(n)

programme.desc: A shark is captured by divers and put on display in a Florida marine centre, where it ultimately dies. However, examination of the body reveals the seemingly fully-grown shark was actually a baby - and its enormous, ferocious mother soon comes looking for it. Thriller sequel, originally released in cinemas in 3D, starring Dennis Quaid, Simon MacCorkindale, Louis Gossett Jr and Bess Armstrong(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath(n)

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian returns to discover the big stories and untold tales behind Scotland's beloved spots, with privileged access to some of the country's most famous locations. In a warm, light-hearted but always informative journey, she looks in-depth at the history, characters and treasures hidden in these stunning spots. In the first episode, Susan explores the wilds of the Cairngorms. Covering an area twice the size of the Lake District, the Cairngorms is Britain's largest National Park, bursting with scenery, history and heritage(n)

programme.title: Holidaying with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer, with the help of a rotating cast of famous guests, travels the globe to sample dream holidays. Take That's Mark Owen is annoyed by a bogus lawyer, and football manager Jose Mourinho stages an elaborate joke. programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Feature-length film taking viewers on a journey aboard a Stanier Class 5 4-6-0 locomotive from the foothills of Ben Nevis down the West Highland route to meet the sea at the port of Mallaig. The programme is with train driver Ian Riley and train guard and manager Florence MacClean for the length of the route as the steam train winds its way past lochs, mountains and the world-famous Glenfinnan viaduct, all the while taking in the beautiful ancient vistas of indigenous ferns, heather and bracken(n)

programme.title: Tony Robinson's History of Britain
programme.desc: The actor explores the history of the nation through the everyday lives of ordinary people, beginning by looking at life during the reign of Tudor king Henry VIII. Tony talks about the role of executioner Thomas Grimes, and reveals how Richard Roose rose from pot washer to being the cook for the Bishop of Rochester(n)

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Michael Buerk returns with more stories of how Britain was transformed during the Victorian era, beginning by looking at the development of the railway network during the era. Michael hops on board an original Victorian locomotive at Tanfield Railway and discovers the role this former coal track played in the development of the national railway. He also learns about Isambard Kingdom Brunel's bid to secure Bristol's future, and his creation of the fastest engine in the world.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Factories
programme.desc: The Kellogg's factory in Manchester opens its doors to reveal how some of the nation's favourite breakfast cereals are made. The only hope of defeating the vengeful creature is to find a magical suit of armour belonging to the boy's father - but finding it will entail matching wits with an assortment of monstrous foes. Animated adventure, with the voices of Charlize Theron, Art Parkinson and Matthew McConaughey(n)

programme.title: Flushed Away (2006)
programme.desc: A pampered pet rodent is flushed down a toilet and ends up in a vast underground rat city in the sewers of London. He is befriended by a quick-witted rat who offers to help him find his way home, but first the hapless pair have to escape the clutches of an evil toad bent on ridding the rodent metropolis of its residents. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Kate Winslet, Ian McKellen, Shane Richie, Jean Reno and Bill Nighy(n)
